message:
fix: update all callers for fallible FcpsFrame::encode and SignedFcpsFrame::new

Follow-up to 7d9e3776 which changed FcpsFrame::encode() from returning
Vec<u8> to Result<Vec<u8>, FrameError> and SignedFcpsFrame::new() to
return Result. All call sites across the codebase now handle the Result:

fcp-protocol/src/fcps.rs (8 test call sites):
- encode_decode_roundtrip, exceeds_mtu, signed_frame_sign_verify,
  signed_frame_wrong_key, signed_frame_roundtrip, empty_symbols_frame,
  single_symbol_roundtrip, validate_frame_lengths — all updated to
  .expect("encode") / .expect("sign").

fcp-protocol/benches/fcps_parsing.rs:
- bench_frame_encode and bench_frame_decode: .expect("encode").

fcp-protocol/tests/fcps_golden_vectors.rs:
- golden_full_frame_roundtrip, reject_frame_exceeding_mtu: .expect("encode").

fcp-protocol/tests/no_mock_integration.rs:
- fcps_frame_encode_decode_roundtrip: .expect("encode").
- signed_frame_sign_and_verify, signed_frame_wrong_key_fails: .expect("sign").

fcp-mesh/degraded.rs:
- DegradedModeEncoder::encode_and_sign: collect signed frames as
  Result<Vec<_>, _> and propagate the error with ?, replacing the
  infallible .collect() that was unsound after the API change.

fcp-mesh/gossip.rs:
- Fix inner doc comment `///` → `//` on MAX_IBLT_BYTES_CAP constant
  (inner doc comments on constants generate spurious rustdoc warnings).

README.md:
- Remove absolute file paths from .rch/config.toml links (not useful
  in rendered markdown on GitHub).
- Improve rch troubleshooting wording for artifact retrieval overflow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1441,7 +1441,7 @@ FCP integrates with the broader Agent Flywheel ecosystem:
 In shared multi-agent sessions, offload CPU-heavy Cargo work through `rch` so local machines do not
 turn into compilation bottlenecks. `rch` fails open to local execution if the worker fleet is unavailable; in swarm-style sessions, abort that local fallback instead of letting heavy Cargo work pile onto the workstation unexpectedly.
 
-This repo also carries a project-level [`.rch/config.toml`](/Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/.rch/config.toml) that pins the critical `.beads` transfer excludes in effective `rch` config. If `rch` prints `Remote command finished: exit=0` and only then fails during `Retrieving build artifacts...`, treat that as a tooling or worker-state problem rather than a Cargo failure; keep the retrieval stderr, then retry on a different worker or continue the investigation in `rch` itself.
+This repo also carries a project-level `.rch/config.toml` that pins the critical `.beads` transfer excludes in effective `rch` config. If `rch` prints `Remote command finished: exit=0` and only then fails during `Retrieving build artifacts...`, treat that as a tooling or worker-state problem rather than a Cargo failure; keep the retrieval stderr, then retry on a different worker or continue the investigation in `rch` itself.
 
 ```bash
 # Build the default workspace members (core platform crates)
@@ -1795,7 +1795,7 @@ Honest about what FCP doesn't do yet:
 | `fwc list` returns "missing-host-endpoint" | No `fcp-host` running | Use `fwc list --offline` for workspace manifest data |
 | Connector returns `NotConfigured` | `configure` not called before `invoke` | Call `fwc config schema <connector>` to see required params, then configure |
 | `cargo build` OOMs on macOS | Too many parallel codegen units | Set `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-build` to avoid Cargo lock contention; or use `rch exec -- cargo build` to offload to remote workers |
-| `rch exec -- cargo ...` ends with rsync `overflow ... .beads/recovery_*` / `recv_file_entry` / code 22 | The remote Cargo command may already have succeeded, but artifact retrieval is walking a worker-side `.beads` recovery tree that current excludes did not prune from that worker | Read the remote-command status lines first. If `rch` reports a remote `exit=0`, treat the compile/test itself as successful and the failure as tooling state. This repo mirrors the critical excludes in both `.rchignore` and [`.rch/config.toml`](/Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/.rch/config.toml), but retrieval-side pruning still needs an upstream `rch` fix or worker cleanup. |
+| `rch exec -- cargo ...` ends with rsync `overflow ... .beads/recovery_*` / `recv_file_entry` / code 22 | The remote Cargo command may already have succeeded, but artifact retrieval is still traversing a worker-side `.beads` recovery tree | Read the remote-command status lines first. If `rch` reports a remote `exit=0`, treat the compile/test itself as successful and the failure as tooling state. This repo mirrors the critical excludes in both `.rchignore` and `.rch/config.toml`; if retrieval still overflows, investigate `rch` client version and stale worker state rather than assuming Cargo failed. |
 | Clippy fails with `fcp-async-core` errors | Pre-existing lints in async-core test code | These are upstream; connector code is clean. Run clippy on specific crates: `cargo clippy -p fcp-<crate>` |
 | OAuth token refresh fails | Token expired between materialize and use | For `credential_id` auth, the egress proxy handles refresh. For `access_token`, re-run configure with a fresh token |
 | SSE stream stops without error | Connection idle timeout | The Anthropic SSE parser has a 16 MiB buffer limit and proper CRLF handling. Check network/proxy timeout settings |
